channel.title: Foods on Max's CyberSpace
channel.description: Recent content in Foods on Max's CyberSpace

item.title: Hyderabadi vs Muradabadi Biryani
item.description:
I am living in New Delhi for a month now, and yesterday I got a chance to try the infamous dish, Biryani. In my state, Biryani is not a common dish and cannot be found at the major places, so I don’t know how it tastes and what’s the standard taste for biryani, but the biryani I ate last night was very flavourful and tasty.

There were two types of biryani on the shop; Hyderabadi and Muradabadi. They ask you at the counter that which one would you like. The Muradabadi one was like a pulav with less masalas, whereas the Hyderabadi one was full of masalas. So I decided to mix both of them and the flavours were so good.

item.title: Lassi
item.description:
Lassi is a refreshing drink for hot summers in India. Every state has its unique flavours and way of preparation. It is prepared with yogurt and water.

Ingredients

  • 250g of full fat yogurt
  • Salt and black pepper (according to taste)
  • Dried mint leaves
  • 200ml of water

Instructions

  • Take full fat yogurt in a big jar or mixer and add 200ml of water.
  • Add salt, black pepper and dried mint leaves and mix/whisk it until the foam starts forming at the top and the water is completely mixed with the yogurt.
  • If the drink is too thick you can add more water.
  • It shouldn’t be watery or thick, the consistency should be like milk or smoothie.

Tips

If you have a sweet tooth, replace the salt, pepper and mint with sugar/jaggery.

item.title: Mutton Curry
item.description:
This is my all time favourite and easy recipe to cook mutton curry. The ingredients can be easily found. This is my first time writing recipe/cooking post, if you want to give me any suggestion or feedback, you can find my email at the bottom of this page. In this recipe, I am using 500g mutton.

Ingredients:

  • 500g Mutton
  • 2 Cup Mustard Oil/ Cooking Oil
  • 2 Small to Medium Sized Onion
  • 2 Small to Medium Sized Tomatoes
  • 2 Green Chillies
  • 2 Garlic Cloves
  • 2 Tbsp Yogurt
  • 1 Tbsp Salt
  • 1/2 Turmeric powder
  • 2 Tbsp Kashmiri Red Chilli Powder
  • 2-4 Tbsp Meat Masala
  • 2 Green Cardamom, 2 Cumin and 5-6 Black Pepper

First, clean the mutton with water thoroughly. Make fine paste of Onion, tomatoes, green chillies and garlic. I recommend using mortar pestle for crushing garlic but if you don’t have it, make sure the garlic cloves are finely chopped. Add the fine paste, and turmeric, salt, kashmiri red chilli powder into the mutton. Add yogurt with 1 tbsp of mustard oil and mix it well. Keep the marinated meat aside for 10-15 minutes.
